title: Split Part 5 of the onboarding/rollout flow into two confirmed sub-steps

requirements.text: Split what was previously 'Part 5' of the multi-step flow (the frontend canister upgrade step in the rollout/onboarding wizard or guided flow) into two distinct sub-steps: Part 5a and Part 5b. Each sub-step must have its own dedicated screen or section with a clear title, description of the action to be taken, and a confirmation prompt that the user must explicitly acknowledge before proceeding to the next sub-step.

quotes: Proceed by breaking Part 5 into 2 with confirmation before each

acceptanceCriteria: Part 5 is divided into exactly two sequential sub-steps (Part 5a and Part 5b).
Each sub-step displays its own descriptive content explaining what action is about to be performed.
A confirmation dialog or inline confirmation prompt appears before entering Part 5a, requiring the user to confirm before proceeding.
A second confirmation dialog or inline confirmation prompt appears before entering Part 5b, requiring the user to confirm before proceeding.
Users cannot skip either confirmation — the 'Continue' / 'Proceed' action is disabled until confirmation is given.
The step indicator/progress tracker (if present) reflects the two sub-steps correctly.
Declining or cancelling a confirmation returns the user to the previous state without advancing.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not alter backend logic or data models — this is a frontend-only change.
Preserve all existing step content; only split and add confirmations, do not remove or rewrite step descriptions.
nonGoals: Restructuring any other parts of the flow (Parts 1–4 or Part 6+).
Changing backend canister upgrade logic or Motoko code.
Modifying rollout documentation markdown files (ROLLOUT_STEPS.md, SMOKE_TEST_*.md) unless they are rendered as part of the UI flow.
Adding new features or portal sections unrelated to the step split.
